meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building a SaaS pricing model. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship high-value projects fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; you don't need years of work, you need one high-value SaaS pricing tool. By building a functional, interactive pricing calculator using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate technical prowess and business acumen, proving to clients you can solve their revenue-generating problems immediately.
content.intro:
The blank slate is the freelancer's greatest enemy. You stare at the screen, paralyzed by the lack of 'social proof' or past projects, while high-ticket clients wait for someone who can actually ship. The secret isn't more experience; it's high-velocity output that moves the needle for their business.

By building a complex SaaS pricing module, you stop selling 'web design' and start selling 'revenue architecture.' With the right AI-driven stack, you can transform a theoretical concept into a production-ready asset in under an hour, effectively bypassing the need for a traditional portfolio.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Establish the variables for the SaaS pricing model, such as user tiers, storage limits, and feature gating. This ensures your code is built on a solid business foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing engine that handles tiered subscriptions, feature toggles, and yearly/monthly discount logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a pricing.ts file based on the Antigravity schema provided.'

steps.title: Build the UI Component
steps.description: Create a responsive, high-converting pricing table that visualizes the data structures created in the previous step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Tailwind CSS pricing component with hover effects, 'most popular' badge styling, and mobile-first responsiveness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create PricingTable.tsx and integrate it into the main dashboard view.'

steps.title: Implement State Management
steps.description: Connect the UI to the backend logic so that pricing updates dynamically when users toggle between monthly and annual plans.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook usePricingCalculator that handles state for billing cycles and calculates annual savings automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install lucide-react && claude edit src/hooks/usePricing.ts'

steps.title: Integrate Conversion Analytics
steps.description: Add tracking logic to see how users interact with the pricing table, providing data-driven insights you can show prospective clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add event listeners to the pricing cards that track click-through rates and plan selection to a mock analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add tracking events to the PricingTable component for analytics integration.'

steps.title: Deploy and Showcase
steps.description: Deploy your completed SaaS pricing module to a live environment, creating a 'live demo' link that serves as the centerpiece of your new portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md file that explains the architecture, the business logic, and the conversion-focused design choices of this pricing tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'git add . && git commit -m "feat: complete saas pricing module" && vercel deploy'

pro_tips: Focus on the 'Most Popular' toggle: Clients love seeing how you influence user psychology through UI design.
Always include a 'Contact Us for Enterprise' button; it shows you understand high-ticket B2B sales cycles.
Use mock data that mimics real SaaS benchmarks to show you understand industry standards.
Document the 'Why' behind every design choice in your README; this is what technical leads look for during hiring.
Keep your CSS variables in a single file to allow for instant 'white-labeling' for potential clients.
resources: Tailwind CSS Pricing Component Patterns: https://tailwindui.com/components
content.next_steps: Take the live URL of your deployed pricing module and add it to your LinkedIn featured section. Reach out to three local agencies and offer to build a custom pricing calculator for their top-tier client, using this project as your primary proof of concept.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to solve the 'no portfolio' problem by providing tangible business value. Stop waiting for work and start building the assets that win contracts. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
